manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: serenity, vigilante, bathtub, daybreak, apartment, snowman, prairie, drugstore, stairway.
Your opponent's team words are: psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
The kill words are: cowardice.

        *   Output must be *only* the required format, no extra text.

Actually, if "Pharmacist" is a distractor, guessing it ends the turn. So linking to drugstore is risky if "Pharmacist" is nearby semantically.

Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Provide a single-word clue and a list of target words from my team's list.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Constraints:
        *   Clue must be a single word.
        *   Clue cannot be one of the words in the lists (team, opponent, distractor, kill).
        *   Clue cannot contain parts of the target words (no compound word splitting).
        *   Goal: Connect as many team words as possible without triggering opponent/distractor/kill words.
        *   Output must be *only* the required format, no extra text.

2.  **Analyze the Word Lists:**
    *   **My Team (9 words):** serenity, vigilante, bathtub, daybreak, apartment, snowman, prairie, drugstore, stairway.
    *   **Opponent (8 words):** psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
    *   **Distractor (7 words):** stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
    *   **Kill (1 word):** cowardice.

3.  **Brainstorm Associations:**

    *   *snowman*: winter, cold, snow, ice, frost.

Actually, if "Pharmacist" is a distractor, guessing it ends the turn. So linking to drugstore is risky if "Pharmacist" is nearby semantically.

    *   Meaning: Inside part of something, inner space, inside of a building/vehicle, domestic space, internal structure.

3.  **Evaluate the Input List against 'interior':**
    *   bathtub: Found inside a bathroom (interior of a house). Possible.
    *   daybreak: Time of day. No strong association.

4.  **Select the Top 3:**

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

TARGETS: <TARGETS>

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: serenity, vigilante, bathtub, daybreak, apartment, snowman, prairie, drugstore, stairway.
Your opponent's team words are: psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
The kill words are: cowardice.

CLUE: INTERIOR
TARGETS: apartment, bathtub, stairway

The words apartment, bathtub, stairway were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: serenity, vigilante, daybreak, snowman, prairie, drugstore.
Remaining words for your opponent are: psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Remaining distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
Remaining kill words are: cowardice.

CLUE: PEACE
TARGETS: serenity, prairie, daybreak

The word serenity was guessed correctly. The word pond was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: vigilante, daybreak, snowman, prairie, drugstore.
Remaining words for your opponent are: psychopath, statute, mutt, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Remaining distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
Remaining kill words are: cowardice.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: serenity, vigilante, bathtub, daybreak, apartment, snowman, prairie, drugstore, stairway.
Your opponent's team words are: psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
The kill words are: cowardice.

CLUE: INTERIOR
TARGETS: apartment, bathtub, stairway

The words apartment, bathtub, stairway were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: serenity, vigilante, bathtub, daybreak, apartment, snowman, prairie, drugstore, stairway.
Your opponent's team words are: psychopath, statute, mutt, enzyme, pond, census, hepatitis, egghead.
Distractor words are: stewardess, blimp, appetizer, pharmacist, prom, geisha, shaman.
The kill words are: cowardice.

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
